author: toaborhese
author_bio: CTO @DataflowLabs | Building AI-powered search | Ex-Google | Stanford CS
original_text: Looking for a senior AI/ML engineer to join our team. Must have experience with LLMs and retrieval systems. Remote OK, US-based preferred. DM me.

pipeline_result.checkpoint: Target senior AI/ML engineers currently or recently employed at DataflowLabs' direct competitors: Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Vectara, Glean, Coveo, or Elasticsearch. These are engineers with hands-on RAG pipeline, dense/sparse hybrid retrieval, and LLM integration experience at the exact product depth DataflowLabs is building. US-based (or remote-eligible) senior/staff-level ICs are the bullseye — especially anyone who has shipped a retrieval or embedding layer in production.

profile.name: @toaborhese
profile.pain_context: CTO of an AI search startup actively scaling their core engineering team — specifically hunting for senior AI/ML talent with LLM + retrieval system depth. This signals they are at a product-building inflection point: the retrieval architecture (likely RAG or hybrid dense/sparse search) is central to their product and they need senior IC ownership, not just generalist ML. Ex-Google background suggests high hiring bar; posting on Twitter rather than LinkedIn implies urgency or a preference for community-native technical talent.
geo_hints: San Francisco Bay Area (probable — Ex-Google + Stanford CS pedigree)

pipeline_result.reply_text:
pulled 50 senior AI/ML engineers from Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Glean etc — all with production RAG/retrieval experience, US-based. some have shipped embedding layers you'd probably want to poach 👀
https://app.lessie.ai/search/mcp_b94728f9cd4f

author: lenaboroditsky
author_bio: Head of Growth @LinguaFlow | Language tech | Previously @Duolingo
original_text: Anyone know content creators who focus on language learning tools? We need reviewers for our new translation feature launch in Latin America.

pipeline_result.checkpoint: Find bilingual (Spanish/Portuguese + English) content creators in Latin America — Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Colombia, Chile — who actively review or demo language learning apps like Duolingo, Babbel, Busuu, or Memrise. Target micro to mid-tier KOLs (20K–500K followers) on YouTube, TikTok, or Instagram who post tutorials, app comparisons, or 'learn English/Spanish with me' content in Spanish or Portuguese. Prioritize creators with high comment engagement and a history of sponsored or organic language app reviews.

profile.name: Lena Boroditsky
profile.pain_context: Launching a new translation feature targeting Latin America and needs language-learning content creators to act as reviewers and amplifiers. As Head of Growth, she is running a KOL/influencer seeding campaign pre- or at-launch — classic growth motion to build social proof and regional awareness before broader paid acquisition.

pipeline_result.reply_text:
ran a search for bilingual content creators in LATAM who review language apps — got 50 across YouTube/TikTok/IG, mostly Spanish & Portuguese speakers doing app comparisons and tutorials. solid mix of micro to mid-tier with real engagement 🎯
https://app.lessie.ai/search/mcp_9c4d2029b81e

author: marcustech_
author_bio: CEO @PayStack | YC W24 | Building the Stripe for Africa
original_text: Hiring a product designer for our fintech startup. Need someone who's worked on payment flows and compliance UX. NYC preferred but open to remote.

pipeline_result.checkpoint: Search for senior product designers with verifiable fintech UX portfolios — specifically payment flow design (checkout, onboarding, KYC/KYB screens) and compliance-heavy UX (identity verification, AML friction reduction). Target designers who have shipped at Flutterwave, Stripe, Chipper Cash, Interswitch, Nala, Moov, or similar African/emerging-market payment infra companies. Prioritize NYC-based or remote-friendly candidates with 4–8 years experience; open to diaspora designers bridging US ↔ West Africa markets.

profile.name: Marcus
profile.pain_context: Early-stage CEO (YC W24) actively scaling product team — hiring a designer who can own payment flow UX and navigate compliance/KYC friction, likely because they're hitting regulatory complexity across African markets and need to professionalize checkout/onboarding UX fast. Posting publicly signals urgency and limited recruiting bandwidth.
